Codes from chapter 15 and sequencing pri.rule: Chapter 15 codes (O00-O9A) have sequencing priority over other chapters.
Codes from chapter 15 and sequencing pri.incidental_pregnancy: If pregnancy is incidental, use Z33.1 instead of Chapter 15 codes.

General Rules for Obstetric Cases.Chapter 15 codes used only on the matern: Never assign Chapter 15 codes on newborn records.

Final character for trimester.rule: Most Chapter 15 codes have a final character for trimester based on provider documentation of trimester or weeks.
Final character for trimester.childbirth_option: If delivery occurs and an 'in childbirth' code is available, assign it.

General Rules for Obstetric Cases.Selection of trimester for inpatient adm: Assign trimester based on when complication developed or admission occurred for pre-existing conditions.
General Rules for Obstetric Cases.Unspecified trimester: Use only when trimester cannot be determined and clarification is not possible.

rules: Assign '0' if fetus affected cannot be determined clinically or by documentation.

General Rules for Obstetric Cases.Completed weeks of gestation: Use full completed weeks; e.g., 39 weeks and 6 days = 39 weeks.

Selection of OB Principal or First-liste.Routine outpatient prenatal visits: Use Z34 for normal pregnancy supervision; do not use with Chapter 15 codes.

Supervision of High-Risk Pregnancy.prenatal_only: O09 is for prenatal period only.
Supervision of High-Risk Pregnancy.labor_delivery: If complications occur, assign applicable Chapter 15 codes; otherwise use O80.

Selection of OB Principal or First-liste.Episodes when no delivery occurs: Principal diagnosis = main complication necessitating encounter.

When a delivery occurs.rule: Principal diagnosis is the condition most related to the delivery.
When a delivery occurs.cesarean_rule: If cesarean performed due to a condition, that condition is principal.

Selection of OB Principal or First-liste.Outcome of delivery: Assign a Z37 code for every maternal record with delivery.

Pre-existing conditions versus condition.rule: Determine if condition existed prior to pregnancy or developed during pregnancy for correct coding.
Pre-existing conditions versus condition.mixed_categories: Some categories do not distinguish and may be used for either.

Pre-existing hypertension in pregnancy.rule: O10 includes hypertensive heart and kidney disease.
Pre-existing hypertension in pregnancy.secondary_code: Add secondary hypertension code to specify type of heart failure or CKD.

Fetal Conditions Affecting the Managemen.O35_O36_usage: Assign only if fetal condition affects maternal management.
Fetal Conditions Affecting the Managemen.in_utero_surgery: Assign O35 with procedure code; do not use Chapter 16 codes on maternal record.

HIV Infection in Pregnancy, Childbirth,.HIV_related_illness: Principal = O98.7-, followed by illness codes.

Pregnancy, Childbirth, and the Puerperiu.Diabetes mellitus in pregnancy: Assign O24 first, followed by E08-E13 diabetes codes.

Gestational (pregnancy induced) diabetes.exclusions: No other O24 codes with O24.4.

Sepsis and septic shock complicating pre.rule: Assign Chapter 15 sepsis code + infection code; add R65.2- and organ dysfunction codes if severe.

Poisoning, toxic effects, adverse effect.sequence: O9A.2 first, then poisoning/toxic effect/adverse effect/underdosing code, then condition caused.

Normal Delivery, Code O80.criteria: Full-term single healthy infant, no complications.
Normal Delivery, Code O80.resolved_complication: O80 can be used if prior complication resolved.

definitions.peripartum: Last month of pregnancy to 5 months postpartum.

The Peripartum and Postpartum Periods.complications: Postpartum complication = any complication within 6 weeks.
The Peripartum and Postpartum Periods.after_6_weeks: Chapter 15 codes may be used if condition is documented as pregnancy-related.
The Peripartum and Postpartum Periods.post_outside_hospital: Use Z39.0 if routine postpartum care after out-of-hospital delivery.
The Peripartum and Postpartum Periods.pregnancy_cardiomyopathy: O90.3 for cardiomyopathy due to pregnancy in patients without pre-existing heart disease.

Code O94, Sequelae of complication.usage: Use for sequelae after initial complication.
Code O94, Sequelae of complication.timing: May be used anytime after postpartum period.
Code O94, Sequelae of complication.sequence: Follow sequela code with code for sequela condition.

Termination of Pregnancy and Spontaneous.retained_products: O03.4 or O07.4 unless specific complication code applies.
Termination of Pregnancy and Spontaneous.complications: Use Chapter 15 complication codes with O04, O07, O08.

Abuse in a pregnant patient.rule: O9A.3-O9A.5 first, then injury/abuse perpetrator codes.

COVID-19 infection in pregnancy.principal_covid: O98.5- first, then U07.1 + manifestation codes if COVID-19 is admission reason.
COVID-19 infection in pregnancy.incidental_covid: Principal = unrelated reason; O98.5-, U07.1, and manifestations as additional.
